title: Microsoft simplifies Windows Insider program fewer channels, and switching without wiping your device
summary: Microsoft simplifies the Windows Insider program with fewer channels, enabling seamless switching between them without wiping your device. This change aims to make it easier for users to test and experience the latest Windows features.
insight: Easier access to latest Windows features without device reset

title: HWMonitor and CPU-Z developer CPUID breached by unknown attackers cyberattack forced users to download malware instead of valid apps for six hours
summary: CPUID's website was compromised by unknown attackers, redirecting users to malware downloads. This breach occurred for six hours, forcing users to potentially install malicious software. The affected tools included HWMonitor and CPU-Z.
insight: Malware downloads can lead to significant security risks and data loss.

title: Framework founder says that personal computing as we know it is dead vows to keep building computers that you can own at the deepest level
summary: Framework founder Nirav Patel vows to keep building personal computers despite the industry shift towards subscription-based models, promising to preserve personal ownership of computation.
insight: Personal computing ownership under threat from subscription-based business models.

title: French government says it's ditching Windows for Linux country accelerates plans to ditch US-based software in digital sovereignty push
summary: France is accelerating its digital sovereignty plans by adopting Linux over Windows. The country's DINUM announced its exit from Windows in favor of Linux workstations. This shift aims to reduce dependence on US-based software.
insight: France boosts national security with less reliance on US tech

title: Vdura hikes its enterprise SSD pricing, now costs 22.6x more than hard drives the price of a 30TB SSD has climbed 472%
summary: Vdura has increased its enterprise SSD pricing by 472% on average, making a 30TB SSD 22.6 times more expensive than a hard drive. This significant price hike may impact businesses and organizations reliant on SSD storage solutions. The updated pricing reflects shifting market dynamics and supply chain costs.
insight: Enterprise SSD prices surge, challenging data storage budget constraints

title: Cooler Master MWE Bronze V2 230V 650W power supply review: A competent entry-level choice
summary: The Cooler Master MWE Bronze V2 230V 650W is a budget power supply that meets expectations, but its performance is limited by compromised active components and an 85 C bulk capacitor.
insight: Limited performance due to compromised components and heat issues.

title: 30 years of Lexar: What a look inside its R&D labs and factory reveals about its plans for an AI-ready future
summary: Lexar is building storage solutions for today and tomorrow's AI-ready demands. A tour of its R&D labs and production facilities showcased the company's innovation and focus on emerging technologies. This includes the development of high-capacity storage for artificial intelligence applications.
insight: AI-ready storage demands faster, more reliable, and high-capacity solutions

title: Embattled streamer goes viral after playing Red Dead Redemption 2 at 4 FPS i5-8300H and a 1050ti with 4GB setup takes 12 hours to play through the first chapter, would make the game 471 hours long
summary: Danish streamer Mongo TV went viral for playing Red Dead Redemption 2 at 4 FPS on an underpowered setup, taking 12 hours to complete the first chapter.
insight: Poorly optimized gaming systems can drastically extend playthrough times.

title: After jumping 2,200% over the last twelve months, DDR4 spot prices fall 5%, the first decline in nearly a year DDR5 pricing sees some relief in China channel market
summary: DDR4 16Gb spot prices fell 5%, marking the first decline in nearly a year after a 2,200% increase over the last 12 months.
insight: Market correction may signal a shift in DDR4 demand and pricing dynamics.

title: Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 Pro Review: Designed for RAM overclocking
summary: The Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 AIO is designed for RAM overclocking, offering targeted cooling and improved performance. In our review, we tested it with AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D CPU. This unique AIO aims to optimize RAM temperatures and enhance overclocking capabilities.
insight: Optimized RAM cooling boosts overclocking potential and performance.

title: Newbie overclocker destroys $5,000 RTX 5090 Lightning Z GPU they used 'to learn how to solder' practicing newfound hobby goes wrong in the most expensive way imaginable
summary: A $5,000 GPU was damaged by a newbie overclocker trying to solder a resistor, leading to repair costs. The incident highlights the risks of experimenting with high-end electronics without proper knowledge. The owner's inexperience led to a costly mistake.
insight: Lack of expertise can lead to expensive mistakes with high-tech gadgets.

title: Engineer installs 3.5-inch floppy drive in a Tesla modern EV recognizes and runs ancient storage device, even plays an MP3 file from diskette
summary: An engineer successfully integrated a 3.5-inch floppy drive into a Tesla, allowing the vehicle to recognize and play files from the ancient storage device. This was achieved using a simple USB-to-FDD converter. The setup even played an MP3 file from a floppy disk.
insight: Reviving legacy tech can work with modern systems through creative adapters.

title: Bee Write Back A Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W-based DIY writerdeck with 5.5-inch OLED and mechanical keyboard
summary: The Bee Write Back is a DIY writerdeck built with Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W, featuring a 5.5-inch OLED screen and mechanical keyboard, designed as a distraction-free writing machine.
insight: Combines digital writing with tactile experience for focused journaling.

title: Perfect Orion splashdown caps record Artemis II mission around the Moon
summary: The Orion spacecraft successfully completed its Artemis II mission, splashing down perfectly and setting a record for a lunar flyby. Sensors played a crucial role in various aspects of the mission, including launch, image capture, and radiation monitoring. The successful mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.
insight: Advanced sensor technology enables safer and more reliable space exploration.

title: Why IoT platforms must evolve for edge intelligence
summary: Edge AI is transforming IoT devices with real-time processing capabilities, enabling them to perform complex tasks autonomously. This shift is driven by the need for faster decision-making and improved efficiency. As a result, IoT platforms must adapt to support edge intelligence.
insight: IoT devices require adaptive platforms for real-time edge intelligence.

title: Artemis II crew splashdown set for at 8:07 p.m. EDT Friday
summary: The Artemis II crew is set to splashdown on Friday at 8:07 p.m. EDT after spending 10 days on a historic mission looping around the Moon and traveling farther into space than ever before. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program. The crew's success sets the stage for future lunar missions.
insight: Human spaceflight beyond low Earth orbit is now a reality again.

title: Florida launches probe into OpenAI as company eyes massive IPO
summary: Florida launches an investigation into OpenAI's data and AI systems. The probe aims to determine if sensitive information could be accessed by adversaries. This is amid OpenAI's plans for a massive IPO.
insight: National security concerns threaten OpenAI's public listing ambitions.

title: South Korea moves to curb the meteoritic rise of DRAM and PC hardware prices
summary: South Korea is working to control rising DRAM and PC hardware prices. Strict market regulation may be necessary but not favored in Washington. Pressure from AI demand is driving up component costs.
insight: Rising AI demand drives hardware price inflation and supply chain strain.

title: Keychron shares 3D keyboard blueprints on GitHub, opening hardware to modders
summary: Keychron has shared 3D keyboard blueprints on GitHub for modders to customize.
insight: Increased user control and customizability in keyboard hardware design.

title: Nvidia's mythical N1 SoC surfaces on a real motherboard, and it's packing 128GB of LPDDR5X
summary: Nvidia's long-rumored N1 SoC has been spotted on a real motherboard with 128GB of LPDDR5X. High-quality images suggest this product is closer to reality. The exact features and specifications of the N1 chip remain unclear.
insight: Nvidia's N1 chip could revolutionize mobile, AI, and gaming performance

title: Tesla is working on a smaller, cheaper electric SUV
summary: Tesla is developing a smaller, more affordable electric SUV in China, with potential US and European production. The new vehicle will be shorter than the Model [Y], measuring around 14 feet in length. Its production aims to make EVs more accessible to a wider audience.
insight: Tesla's new model seeks to make electric vehicles more affordable.

title: DDR5 prices drop nearly 30%, but memory costs are still far from normal
summary: DDR4 chip prices have corrected slightly, but costs remain elevated after a prolonged increase.
insight: DRAM price fluctuations impact memory cost stability and technology adoption.

title: Amazon laid off 30,000 workers while CEO Andy Jassy got a 30% pay bump
summary: Amazon's CEO Andy Jassy received a 30% pay bump despite laying off 30,000 workers. The company published its annual proxy statement revealing Jassy's compensation for last year. This move has sparked controversy among employees and critics.
insight: CEO compensation often diverges from company's worker treatment decisions

title: FBI recovers "deleted" Signal messages through iPhone notifications
summary: The FBI recovered deleted Signal messages from an iPhone by accessing push notifications stored in a separate database.
insight: FBI can recover deleted data through iPhone's notification database access.

title: PC market posts modest growth in early 2026 despite memory shortages and economic strain
summary: The PC market saw modest growth in early 2026 despite economic strain and memory shortages. Preemptive purchasing and upgrades contributed to the uptick. Vendors and customers made cautious moves ahead of expected price hikes.
insight: Preemptive purchasing mitigated the impact of memory shortages financially.

title: VeraCrypt, WireGuard among projects disrupted by Microsoft account suspensions
summary: VeraCrypt's development is disrupted due to Microsoft's account suspension of its developer.
insight: Microsoft's actions impact open-source security projects and developers worldwide.

title: Microsoft OneDrive users report mysterious spam files that won't go away
summary: Microsoft OneDrive users report receiving suspicious shared files and links in December, sparking concerns about phishing or hacking attempts.
insight: User data security is compromised due to unexplained OneDrive spam files.

title: Intel tops $300 billion market cap for the first time since the dot-com boom
summary: Intel surpasses $300 billion market capitalization for the first time since the dot-com boom, reversing its decline and regaining its position as a leader in the tech industry.
insight: Intel's resurgence demonstrates its sustained competitiveness in the industry.

title: The cables powering the internet are under the ocean and under threat
summary: A surge in submarine-cable construction is driven by AI demand for increased network capacity.
insight: Rising demand for AI requires more undersea internet connectivity options.
